topic: A close-up of a 'Vinyl Record Player' being started.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: front view: Power switch toggled on; the turntable's platter remains still.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: close-up: Platter begins to spin as the motor engages and the record edge comes into frame.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: top-down: The stylus settles into the groove and the record spins smoothly.
parsed_generation.narration: Power flows from switch to spin as the turntable comes to life. The sequence tightens from a frontal view to an intimate close-up and finally a top-down look at the stylus riding the groove.

feedback: The sequence clearly shows a vinyl turntable starting, with distinct shot angles and timestamps. However, it omits the iconic macro moment of placing the record or lowering the tonearm, and misses the crackle cue that anchors the gold standard. To align more closely, add a macro-wide shot of the record on the platter with a hand lowering the tonearm and a brief audible crackle as the music begins.
fixes: Include hand interaction lowering the tonearm onto the groove
Add a macro shot of the record on the platter for macro emphasis
Incorporate audible cues (crackle) or SFX to match gold standard
